label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The question is asking how the researchers found out that the dogs understood the woman's intention. The options are A through D. Looking back at the text, the key part is when the researchers did the study with the dogs. The text says the dogs were shown a movie where a woman looked at them and said "Hi dog!" Then she looked at a flowerpot. The researchers found that when the woman looked at and spoke directly to the dog, the dog followed her eyes to the flowerpot. This proves the dogs knew the woman was thinking about the flowerpot. The question is about how the researchers found this out. The text says, "By following the eye movements of dogs, we were able to get a first-hand look at how their minds are actually working," said Jozsef, the senior researcher. This directly connects the method of tracking eye movements to understanding the dogs' comprehension. So the answer should be B, by reading their eye movements. The other options don't fit. A is something the woman did, not the method
